prompt: A highly realistic scene inside a traditional tailoring workshop. A tailor is obsessively crafting an extremely intricate suit on a mannequin. The suit is over-engineered, covered with excessive stitching, patches, and adjustments, precisely matching every tiny irregularity and imperfection of the mannequin’s surface. The mannequin itself is slightly warped and asymmetrical, and the suit fits it perfectly, conforming to every flaw. In the background, several other mannequins with normal proportions stand nearby, but the suit would clearly not fit them at all. The workshop is grounded and physical, with fabric rolls, scissors, threads, and wooden tables. Natural lighting, realistic textures, no stylization. The scene feels like a photograph taken in the real world. No text, no labels, no diagrams, no symbols, no glowing effects, no abstract elements, no futuristic or sci-fi style.
metaphor: A suit tailored perfectly to one flawed mannequin captures every imperfection, making it unusable for other normal mannequins.
